flashcards.back: A computer system with one main CPU capable of executing a general-purpose instruction set. It may have special-purpose processors (disk controllers, GPUs) but only one general-purpose CPU.
A system with two or more CPUs sharing the computer bus, clock, memory, and peripheral devices. Also known as parallel systems or tightly coupled systems.

flashcards.front: What are the three main advantages of multiprocessor systems?
flashcards.back:
1. Increased Throughput - more work done in less time
2. Economy of Scale - sharing peripherals and storage is cheaper than separate systems
3. Increased Reliability - failure of one processor does not halt the system (graceful degradation)

flashcards.front: What is the difference between SMP and AMP?
flashcards.back:
SMP (Symmetric Multiprocessing): All processors are peers and perform all tasks equally, running the same OS.
AMP (Asymmetric Multiprocessing): A master processor controls the system and assigns tasks to slave processors.

flashcards.front: What is graceful degradation in multiprocessor systems?
flashcards.back: The ability to continue providing service proportional to the level of surviving hardware. If one processor fails, the remaining processors take over its work, maintaining system operation at reduced capacity.
A system composed of two or more individual computer systems (nodes) coupled together via a LAN, sharing storage and working together. They provide high availability by monitoring each other.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between asymmetric and symmetric clustering?
flashcards.back:
Asymmetric clustering: One node is in hot-standby mode monitoring the active server; takes over if the active fails.
Symmetric clustering: All nodes run applications and monitor each other; more efficient as it uses all hardware.

flashcards.front: What is fault tolerance and how does it differ from graceful degradation?
flashcards.back: Fault tolerance goes beyond graceful degradation by allowing a system to suffer a failure of any single component and still continue operation. It requires failure detection, diagnosis, and correction mechanisms.

Processors with multiple computing cores on a single chip. They are more efficient than multiple single-core chips because on-chip communication is faster and uses less power than between-chip communication.
A blade server consists of multiple processor boards, I/O boards, and networking boards placed in the same chassis. Each blade-processor board boots independently and runs its own operating system.
